title: Textarea Item
description: A multi-line string-family variable widget. It binds one document/container variable (the `variable` it sets) and lets a viewer type a multi-line free-text value; on change the value flows through the resolver's override path and re-resolves the document so dependent `${var}`/`$var` consumers update live. A textarea may only bind a variable whose declared type is `string` — the resolver enforces this widget↔variable type compatibility and reports WIDGET_TYPE_MISMATCH otherwise. The variable itself is declared in the document/container `variables`, which supplies the effective default; this config only declares the binding plus its presentation. A textarea never has children.

variable.description: Name of the variable this widget sets. Must be a variable visible in the widget's scope (declared at document scope or on an ancestor container) and declared with a type the widget's family permits (string family → string). The value entered here becomes that variable's runtime override on re-resolve.

label.description: Optional label rendered next to the control.

description.description: Optional help text describing what the input controls, rendered beneath or beside the control.

default.description: Optional widget-local default shown before the viewer edits the field. The variable's declared default remains the authoritative resolution-time value; this is presentation only.

disabled.description: When true the control is rendered read-only and cannot be edited.

placeholder.description: Optional placeholder text shown in the empty field.
